rollcall-2022.name: Roll Call - Covering Capitol Hill Since 1955

children.name: Trucks and cars cross over the Rio Grande and U.S.-Mexico border on the Bridge of the Americas in El Paso, Texas, in 2021. (Bill Clark/CQ Roll Call file photo)

Lower court rulings are thwarting Department of Homeland Security efforts to focus its limited resources, the Biden administration argues

Pennsylvania Lt. Gov. John Fetterman, right, hasn't been on the campaign trail since he suffered a stroke just before winning the Democratic primary for the state's open Senate seat. He spoke in April at a union hall in Plymouth Meeting, Pa., with, from left, Maggie Birge-Caracappa, Janice Rael, and Blake Cash. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call file photo)
August date to watch in Pennsylvania Senate race

A memorandum written by House Oversight and Reform Chairwoman Carolyn B. Maloney was used in court Friday to justify the committee’s investigation to uphold parts of the subpoena. (Bill Clark/CQ Roll Call file photo)
Appeals court lets House panel subpoena Trump financial records

Senate Majority Leader Charles E. Schumer, left, and Minority Leader Mitch McConnell attend a ceremony on June 8 to announce the renaming of Senate rooms in the Capitol after former Sens. Barbara Mikulski and Margaret Chase Smith. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call file photo)

Texas Democratic Rep. Sheila Jackson Lee attends a news conference on the treatment of Haitian migrants at the southwest border last year. (Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images file photo)

President Joe Biden signs an executive order on access to reproductive health care services as Vice President Kamala Harris, left, and Secretary of Health and Human Services Xavier Becerra look on in the Roosevelt Room of the White House on Friday. (Alex Wong/Getty Images)
Biden issues executive order responding to abortion ruling
This week’s Hits and Misses focuses on Louisiana Sen. John Kennedy and some of his homespun witticisms. (Photo by Bill Clark, composite by Chris Hale/CQ Roll Call)

Arizona Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly, center, left, and Rep. Tom O’Halleran, center, right, talk as they arrive for the Flagstaff Chamber of Commerce Fourth of July parade in Flagstaff, Ariz., on Monday. (Bill Clark/CQ Roll Call)

President Joe Biden presents the Presidential Medal of Freedom to former Rep. Gabrielle Giffords, D-Ariz., one of 17 recipients to receive the nation's highest civilian honor, during a ceremony at the White House on Thursday. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call)

Activists listen during a news conference marking the 10th anniversary of Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals, or DACA, on Capitol Hill in June. (Kent Nishimura / Los Angeles Times via Getty Images)

In this photo provided by the National Park Service, the Yellowstone National Park's North Entrance Road is washed out after flooding on June 16. (Jacob W. Frank/National Park Service via Getty Images file photo)

The summons for Sen. Lindsey Graham, filed in Fulton County Superior Court on Tuesday, is one of more than half a dozen issued for allies of former President Donald Trump. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call file photo)

A group hoping to boost voter registration is encouraging people to hold dinners with friends, neighbors and family members in the belief it will be more effective than strangers with clipboards going door-to-door or standing on streetcorners. (Caroline Brehman/CQ Roll Call file photo)

“No more drilling, including offshore,” President Joe Biden said at a campaign debate. Diane Hoskins, Oceana campaign director, said the possibility the administration might allow drilling was disappointing. (AFP via Getty Images)

A supporter of then-President Donald Trump holds a Confederate flag outside the Senate chamber during the Jan. 6, 2021 Capitol riot. (SAUL LOEB/AFP/via Getty Images)

EPA Administrator Michael S. Regan may find a narrow path for his agency's efforts to rein in climate change. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call file photo)

Sen. Patrick J. Leahy, D-Vt., is the longest-serving sitting senator and chairman of the Appropriations Committee. He will retire at the end of the 117th Congress. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call file photo)

Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell speaks at a news conference after a luncheon in the U.S. Capitol on June 22. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call)

The case was brought by states that argue the EPA overstepped its bounds in regulating carbon emissions by power plants. (Getty Images)

“First on gun safety, then on abortion, and now on the environment – this MAGA, regressive, extremist Supreme Court is intent on setting America back decades, if not centuries,” Senate Majority Leader Charles E. Schumer, D-N.Y., said of the Supreme Court's ruling in West Virginia v. EPA. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call)

Cardboard cutouts of the conservative Supreme Court justices are propped up by abortion rights activists in front of the court before the decision to overturn Roe v. Wade was handed down last week. (Bill Clark/CQ Roll Call)

Former White House counsel Pat Cipollone was subpoenaed by the Jan. 6 committee. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call file photo)

The drug pricing negotiations aim to resuscitate part of Democrats’ sweeping social spending and climate bill, with Senate Majority Leader Charles E. Schumer, D-N.Y., negotiating with the broader bill’s most vocal Democratic holdout, Sen. Joe Manchin III of West Virginia on the drug pricing provisions. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call)

Rep. Frank Pallone, seen here with Speaker Nancy Pelosi, has called for renewal of a ban on crude oil exports. (Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call file photo)

Immigration activists demonstrate in front of the Supreme Court on April 26 as the justices hear oral arguments about whether President Joe Biden can end a Trump-era border policy that denies asylum-seekers entry to the U.S. while their case is reviewed. (Bill Clark/CQ Roll Call file photo)
